# Validator Engine (scripts + schemas)

Covers the Python 3.8+ check engine that enforces rules R1‚ÄìR41 over the YAML artifacts the skill emits, the 11 JSON Schemas that mirror it, and the acceptance/failure-mode test specs.

## Core Invariants

- `scripts/checks/__init__.py` is the ONLY place enums, regexes, and required-field tuples live ‚Äî every rule module imports its constants from there [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:35, skills/create-loop/scripts/checks/__init__.py:1-9].
- The Python validator is authoritative; `schemas/*.json` MUST mirror it. They must never disagree on any enum or required field [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:43].
- Validator scripts are READ-ONLY: they load YAML, print violations to stderr, and never mutate an artifact [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:3, 42].
- A nonzero exit from `check_loop_integrity.py` means enter a recovery subgraph ‚Äî do NOT advance the loop [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:37].
- Rule numbers (Rn) are the cross-citation language across SKILL.md, references, schemas, and tests ‚Äî never renumber, only append [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:36].

## Conventions

- Exit codes are uniform: `0` valid, `1` structural error, `2` load/usage error [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:3].
- Adding a gate/node/status token: edit the frozensets/tuples in `checks/__init__.py` FIRST, then the consuming rule module, then keep `schemas/*.json` in lockstep [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:35].
- One concern per rule module under `checks/` (e.g., `graph.py` = R1 cycle/R2 dangling, `gates.py` = R3/R7/R34) [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:14-20].
- After changing any validator or schema, run `tests/failure_mode_tests.md` (rejection fixtures) plus the acceptance gate [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:38].
- Run `check_loop_integrity.py` at session start, after every node completion, and after every plan mutation [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:30].

## Common Mistakes

- Hard-coding an enum inline in a rule module instead of importing from `checks/__init__.py` ‚Äî this defeats the single-source-of-truth guarantee and lets enums diverge [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:41].
- Making a script mutate an artifact ‚Äî the scripts are strictly read-only checks [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:42].
- Letting `validate_loop_plan.py` and the JSON Schemas disagree on an enum/required field [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:43].

## Verified Facts

- Four top-level scripts: `validate_loop_plan.py` (dispatcher over 10 `--kind` values), `validate_checkpoint.py` (checkpoint‚Üîplan, R6/R19/R20/R22/R33), `check_loop_integrity.py` (whole-loop-dir gate), `render_dag.py` (plan ‚Üí Mermaid + Graphviz DOT) [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:8-11].
- `NODE_STATUSES` is a 15-member frozenset [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/checks/__init__.py:15-19].
- `SUBGRAPH_STATUSES` is an 8-member frozenset, terminal states completed/failed/promoted_to_subloop/cancelled, disjoint from node statuses [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/checks/__init__.py:31-37].
- `PLAN_REQUIRED` = 12 fields; `NODE_REQUIRED` = 21 fields (incl. `child_loops`); `LEDGER_REQUIRED` = 2; `LOOP_META_REQUIRED` = 12 [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/checks/__init__.py:76-100].
- `LOOP_ID_RE = ^L\d{3}(\.\d{2})*$`; `SLUG_RE = ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$`; `SLUG_MAX_LEN = 32` [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/checks/__init__.py:61-65].
- `GATE_REQUIRED_KINDS` = {milestone, gate, mapper, branch, join}; fanout/compensation may be gate-exempt, approval must carry a human_approval gate (R34) [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/checks/__init__.py:72-74].
- `jsonschema` is optional; scripts degrade gracefully without it, PyYAML is mandatory [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:3].
- `validate_loop_plan.py --kind` accepts: loop_plan, node_contract, evidence_ledger, loop_meta, loops_index, node_runtime, claim, event_log, loop_state, artifact_index [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:26-27].

## Working Understanding

- `check_loop_integrity.py` appears to run the per-file validators as subprocesses AND add cross-file reconciliation (checkpoint‚Üîplan‚Üîledger‚Üîindex, completed-needs-active-evidence, evidence-artifact-exists) ‚Äî making it the anti-corruption gate for long-running loops [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/AGENTS.md:37].
- The `human_intervention_policy` (HIP) enums are for an OPTIONAL top-level plan field (rule R18); a plan without it stays valid [source: skills/create-loop/scripts/checks/__init__.py:41-56].
